test(bundle): add comprehensive unit tests for generator (bd-2hj9x)

Tests cover:
- Generator creation and file addition
- Scrollback with line limiting
- Zip and tar.gz archive generation
- Size limit enforcement
- Manifest integrity verification
- Round-trip generation and verification
- Redaction summary tracking
- Output path suggestions

All tests use temp directories and don't depend on tmux.

 {"id":"bd-2hjn","title":"Task: Create ContextPredictor module","description":"## Purpose
Implement context window exhaustion prediction based on token velocity tracking.

## Module Location
internal/context/predictor.go

## ContextPredictor Struct
type ContextPredictor struct {
    samples      []TokenSample
    window       time.Duration    // Velocity averaging window
    pollInterval time.Duration
}

type TokenSample struct {
    Timestamp time.Time
    Tokens    int
}

type Prediction struct {
    CurrentUsage         float64
    TokenVelocity        float64  // tokens per minute
    MinutesToExhaustion  float64
    ShouldWarn           bool
    ShouldCompact        bool
}

## Key Methods
func (p *ContextPredictor) AddSample(tokens int)
func (p *ContextPredictor) PredictExhaustion(modelLimit int) *Prediction
func (p *ContextPredictor) calculateVelocity() float64

## Prediction Logic
velocity = (latest_tokens - oldest_tokens_in_window) / window_duration
remaining = model_limit - current_tokens
minutes_to_exhaustion = remaining / velocity

ShouldWarn = minutes < 15 && usage > 0.70
ShouldCompact = minutes < 8 && usage > 0.75

## Acceptance Criteria
- Accurately tracks token velocity
- Handles bursts and pauses in output
- Returns nil Prediction when insufficient data
- Thread-safe for concurrent access

## Technical Notes
Use ring buffer for sample storage
Consider exponential moving average for smoothing","notes":"## Testing Requirements

 {"id":"bd-2hyj","title":"Task: Integrate with ntm assign","description":"## Purpose
Use effectiveness scores to improve agent assignment decisions.

## Scope
- Feed historical scores into assignment algorithm as a **soft prior**
- Weight task-agent matching by past performance (per task type)
- Support "learning mode" vs "exploitation mode"

## Algorithm Guidelines
- **Min samples threshold** before using scores (avoid cold-start bias)
- **Decay** older scores (recent performance weighted higher)
- **Deterministic ranking** for same inputs
- **Explainability**: include score components in assignment rationale

## Acceptance Criteria
- Assignment considers historical effectiveness
- Can toggle learning mode (try new combinations)
- Explanation shows why agent was chosen
- Override possible for manual control
- No regression: assignment works when scores missing

## Tests
- Unit: ranking with/without score data
- Unit: min-sample gating + decay
- E2E: verify assignment decisions change with score history

## Technical Notes
- Depends on bd-9tbv (BV Task Assignment) being complete
- Consider multi-armed bandit approach for exploration/exploitation","notes":"## Testing Requirements

 {"id":"bd-2ztw9","title":"Task: Implement synthesis strategy definitions and selection logic","description":"Implement synthesis strategy definitions and selection logic.

## Strategy Set (canonical)
- manual (mechanical merge only; no synthesizer agent)
- adversarial
- consensus
- creative
- analytical
- deliberative
- prioritized
- dialectical (agent-led debate synthesis)
- meta-reasoning (meta-cognitive synthesizer)
- voting (structured score/vote aggregation)
- argumentation-graph (support/attack map)

## Required Code Changes
- Update `SynthesisStrategy` enum in internal/ensemble/types.go to the canonical set.
- Update validation (`IsValid`) and any existing usages.
- Provide mapping or migration if older names exist (fail fast with clear error if not supported).

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/strategy.go
type SynthesisStrategy string

const (
    StrategyManual         SynthesisStrategy = "manual"
    StrategyAdversarial    SynthesisStrategy = "adversarial"
    StrategyConsensus      SynthesisStrategy = "consensus"
    StrategyCreative       SynthesisStrategy = "creative"
    StrategyAnalytical     SynthesisStrategy = "analytical"
    StrategyDeliberative   SynthesisStrategy = "deliberative"
    StrategyPrioritized    SynthesisStrategy = "prioritized"
    StrategyDialectical    SynthesisStrategy = "dialectical"
    StrategyMetaReasoning  SynthesisStrategy = "meta-reasoning"
    StrategyVoting         SynthesisStrategy = "voting"
    StrategyArgumentation  SynthesisStrategy = "argumentation-graph"
)

type StrategyConfig struct {
    Name            SynthesisStrategy
    Description     string
    RequiresAgent   bool
    SynthesizerMode string
    OutputFocus     []string
    BestFor         []string
    TemplateKey     string
}

func GetStrategy(name string) (*StrategyConfig, error)
func ListStrategies() []*StrategyConfig
func ValidateStrategy(name string) bool
```

- [ ] Strategy list returned in deterministic order

 {"id":"bd-3045p","title":"Docs: Robot Mode API Design Principles","description":"## Overview

Document the canonical robot mode API design principles to ensure consistency across all current and future robot commands. This serves as the authoritative reference for contributors implementing new robot mode features.

## Document Location

Create `docs/robot-api-design.md` with the following content.

---

## Robot Mode API Design Principles

### 1. Command Naming Patterns

#### Core Session Operations
Session-scoped commands use `=SESSION` syntax:
```bash
--robot-send=SESSION
--robot-tail=SESSION
--robot-spawn=SESSION
--robot-context=SESSION
--robot-env=SESSION
--robot-probe=SESSION
```

#### Global Commands
Commands that operate globally (no session context) are bool flags:
```bash
--robot-status        # Lists all sessions
--robot-version       # Version info
--robot-plan          # bv global plan
--robot-tools         # Tool inventory
--robot-capabilities  # API discovery
```

#### Tool Bridges
External tool integrations follow `--robot-<tool>-<action>` pattern:
```bash
--robot-jfp-list      # JFP prompt listing
--robot-jfp-search    # JFP search (uses --query=)
--robot-xf-search     # XF archive search (uses --query=)
--robot-dcg-check     # DCG safety check (uses --command=)
--robot-mail-check    # Agent Mail inbox (uses --project=)
--robot-cass-search   # CASS history search
--robot-rano-stats    # rano network stats
--robot-rch-status    # RCH build offload status
```

### 2. Parameter Patterns

#### Use Modifier Flags, NOT Inline Values
Tool bridges should NOT use inline values in the main flag.

**CORRECT:**
```bash
ntm --robot-xf-search --query="rust async" --limit=10
ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="rm -rf /data"
ntm --robot-mail-check --project=myproj --agent=cc_1
```

**INCORRECT:**
```bash
ntm --robot-xf-search="rust async"     # AVOID
ntm --robot-dcg-check="rm -rf /data"   # AVOID
ntm --robot-mail-check=myproj          # AVOID
```

#### Global Shared Modifier Flags
These flags are shared across many commands and should NOT be tool-prefixed:
- `--limit=N` - Max results (not `--xf-limit`)
- `--offset=N` - Pagination offset (not `--mail-offset`)
- `--since=DATE` - Start date filter (not `--cass-since`)
- `--until=DATE` - End date filter (not `--xf-until`)
- `--panes=1,2,3` - Pane filter
- `--lines=N` - Lines to capture
- `--query=Q` - Search query
- `--verbose` - Detailed output (optional, see §6)

#### Tool-Specific Modifier Flags
Only use tool prefix for options unique to that tool:
- `--xf-mode=semantic` - XF-specific search mode
- `--xf-sort=date` - XF-specific sort order
- `--rano-window=10m` - rano-specific time window
- `--probe-method=keystroke_echo` - probe-specific method

### 3. Output Envelope

All robot commands MUST include these fields:
```json
{
  "success": true,         // Required: always first
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",  // Required: when response generated
  "error": "...",          // Only on failure
  "error_code": "...",     // Only on failure, machine-readable
  "hint": "..."            // Recovery suggestion
}
```

#### Standard Error Codes
- `SESSION_NOT_FOUND` - Session does not exist
- `PANE_NOT_FOUND` - Pane does not exist
- `TOOL_NOT_FOUND` - External tool not installed
- `PROJECT_NOT_FOUND` - Project does not exist
- `AGENT_NOT_FOUND` - Agent does not exist
- `THREAD_NOT_FOUND` - Thread does not exist
- `INVALID_FLAG` - Invalid flag combination
- `INVALID_INPUT` - Invalid parameter value
- `MISSING_REQUIRED` - Required parameter missing
- `TIMEOUT` - Operation timed out
- `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` - Feature not yet available
- `PERMISSION_DENIED` - Authorization required
- `INTERNAL_ERROR` - Unexpected internal error

### 4. Exit Codes

| Code | Meaning | When Used |
|------|---------|-----------|
| 0 | Success | Operation completed successfully |
| 1 | Error | Parse error, command failed, invalid input |
| 2 | Unavailable | Tool not installed, NOT_IMPLEMENTED |

**Exit Code Mapping:**
- `TOOL_NOT_FOUND` → exit 2
- `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` → exit 2
- `MISSING_REQUIRED` → exit 1
- `INVALID_FLAG` → exit 1
- `INVALID_INPUT` → exit 1
- `SESSION_NOT_FOUND` → exit 1
- All other errors → exit 1

### 5. Pagination Pattern

Commands that return lists MUST support pagination:

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "total_matches": 150,      // Total available (before limit)
  "offset": 20,              // Current offset
  "count": 10,               // Items in this response
  "has_more": true,          // More items available
  "items": [...],
  "_agent_hints": {
    "next_offset": 30,       // Offset for next page
    "pages_remaining": 12    // Approximate pages left
  }
}
```

**Required Pagination Flags:**
- `--limit=N` - Max items to return (default varies by command, typically 20)
- `--offset=N` - Skip first N items (default 0)

**Required Response Fields:**
- `total_matches` or `total_messages` or similar - Total count before pagination
- `offset` - Current offset echoed back
- `count` - Number of items in current response
- `has_more` - Boolean indicating more results available
- `_agent_hints.next_offset` - Next offset value for convenience
- `_agent_hints.pages_remaining` - Approximate pages left (optional but recommended)

### 6. Verbose Flag Pattern

The `--verbose` flag is a global modifier that increases output detail. **It is optional and should only be implemented when there is meaningful additional detail to provide.**

**When to implement `--verbose`:**
- Safety checks (DCG) - show analysis details, rule explanations
- Status commands - show extended metadata, timing info
- Commands where abbreviated vs detailed output makes sense

**When NOT to implement `--verbose`:**
- Search commands that already return full content (xf-search)
- Commands where all useful information is already in the default output

**Example (DCG without --verbose):**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "allowed": false,
  "severity": "high",
  "rationale": "Destructive command"
}
```

**Example (DCG with --verbose):**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "allowed": false,
  "severity": "high",
  "rationale": "Destructive command",
  "rule_matched": "HARD_RESET",
  "analysis": {
    "command_parsed": ["git", "reset", "--hard"],
    "flags_detected": ["--hard"],
    "risk_factors": ["discards uncommitted changes", "cannot be undone"],
    "affected_files_estimate": 42
  }
}
```

### 7. Array Fields

Critical arrays are ALWAYS present, even if empty:
```json
{
  "sessions": [],    // Empty array, not absent
  "agents": [],      // Empty array, not absent
  "messages": [],    // Empty array, not absent
  "hits": []         // Empty array, not absent
}
```

This allows safe iteration without null checks.

### 8. Optional Fields

Use `omitempty` for optional fields - they are absent when not applicable:
- `error`, `error_code`, `hint` - Only on error
- `_agent_hints` - Only when hints available
- `variant` - Only if agent has model variant
- `body` - Only when `--include-bodies` set

### 9. Agent Hints

Include `_agent_hints` object with actionable suggestions:
```json
{
  "_agent_hints": {
    "summary": "Brief human-readable summary",
    "suggested_action": "What to do next",
    "next_offset": 20,
    "pages_remaining": 5,
    "safer_alternative": "Alternative command if blocked",
    "warnings": ["Any issues to be aware of"]
  }
}
```

### 10. Filter Echo Pattern

Commands with multiple filters SHOULD echo the active filters:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "filters": {
    "status": "unread",
    "urgent_only": true,
    "thread": null,
    "since": "2024-01-01",
    "until": "2024-06-30"
  },
  ...
}
```

This helps agents verify their filters were applied correctly.

### 11. Context/Intent Pattern

Commands that benefit from context SHOULD accept `--context=`:
```bash
ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="rm -rf ./build" --context="Cleaning CI artifacts"
```

Context helps the tool make better decisions and improves error messages.

### 12. Documentation Requirements

Every robot command must document:
1. **Command interface** with examples
2. **Output schema** with JSON example
3. **Error response** with JSON example (including error_code)
4. **Modifier flags** table with scope (global/tool-specific)
5. **Exit codes** (must follow standard)
6. **Unit tests** (80% coverage target)
7. **E2E test script** with log prefix

### 13. Testing Requirements

#### Unit Test Coverage
Every robot command implementation must include unit tests for:
- Flag parsing for all parameters
- Required parameter validation
- Invalid input rejection
- Output JSON serialization
- Timestamp field inclusion
- Error code generation
- Exit code mapping
- Empty result handling
- Pagination calculations (offset, count, has_more, next_offset)
- Filter application
- Date range validation (if --since/--until supported)

#### E2E Test Script Standards
Every robot command must have an E2E test script following this pattern:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# [E2E-XXX] Robot <Command> End-to-End Tests
# =============================================================================
# Tests the --robot-<command> for <description>.
# Exit codes: 0=all passed, 1=test failed, 77=skipped (tool not installed)
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-XXX]"
PASSED=0
FAILED=0
SKIPPED=0

log_info()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [INFO]  $*"; }
log_pass()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [PASS]  $*"; ((PASSED++)); }
log_fail()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [FAIL]  $*"; ((FAILED++)); }
log_skip()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [SKIP]  $*"; ((SKIPPED++)); }
log_debug() { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [DEBUG] $*"; }

cleanup() {
    log_info "Test summary: $PASSED passed, $FAILED failed, $SKIPPED skipped"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

log_info "Starting robot <command> E2E tests"
log_info "Test timestamp: $(date -Iseconds)"

# Prerequisite checks...
# Test cases with IDs (XXX-001, XXX-002, etc.)...
# Final summary...
```

**E2E Test Naming Convention:**
- XF commands: `[E2E-XF]` prefix, test IDs `XF-001`, `XF-002`, etc.
- DCG commands: `[E2E-DCG]` prefix, test IDs `DCG-001`, `DCG-002`, etc.
- MAIL commands: `[E2E-MAIL]` prefix, test IDs `MAIL-001`, `MAIL-002`, etc.
- CASS commands: `[E2E-CASS]` prefix, test IDs `CASS-001`, `CASS-002`, etc.

**Required Test Cases:**
1. Basic successful operation
2. All optional parameters individually
3. Missing required parameters
4. Invalid parameter values
5. Empty results handling
6. Exit code verification (0, 1, 2)
7. Timestamp field presence
8. Error code in error responses
9. Pagination (limit, offset, count, has_more)
10. Combined options
11. Date range validation (if applicable)

### 14. Backward Compatibility

- JSON output is the default and must remain stable
- TOON format is opt-in via `--robot-format=toon`
- Schema changes must be additive (new fields ok, removal requires deprecation)
- Deprecation period: 2 minor versions before removal

 {"id":"bd-w254z","title":"AccountRotator: Detect current account state","description":"## Purpose
Detect when an agent hits usage limits by monitoring pane output and trigger account rotation through caam integration.

## Implementation Details

```go
// AccountRotator manages caam-based account switching on limit detection
type AccountRotator struct {
    caamPath       string           // Path to caam binary
    limitDetector  *LimitDetector
    rotationState  map[string]*RotationState // pane -> state
    mu             sync.RWMutex
    logger         *slog.Logger
}

type RotationState struct {
    CurrentAccount   string
    PreviousAccounts []string
    RotationCount    int
    LastRotation     time.Time
}

// OnLimitHit is called when LimitDetector detects a rate limit
func (ar *AccountRotator) OnLimitHit(event LimitHitEvent) error {
    ar.mu.Lock()
    defer ar.mu.Unlock()
    
    state := ar.getOrCreateState(event.SessionPane)
    
    ar.logger.Info("limit hit detected, initiating account rotation",
        "pane", event.SessionPane,
        "agent_type", event.AgentType,
        "pattern", event.Pattern,
        "current_account", state.CurrentAccount,
        "rotation_count", state.RotationCount)
    
    // Get next available account from caam
    newAccount, err := ar.getNextAccount(event.AgentType)
    if err != nil {
        ar.logger.Error("failed to get next account",
            "agent_type", event.AgentType,
            "error", err)
        return fmt.Errorf("get next account: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Track rotation
    state.PreviousAccounts = append(state.PreviousAccounts, state.CurrentAccount)
    state.CurrentAccount = newAccount
    state.RotationCount++
    state.LastRotation = time.Now()
    
    ar.logger.Info("account rotation prepared",
        "pane", event.SessionPane,
        "old_account", state.PreviousAccounts[len(state.PreviousAccounts)-1],
        "new_account", newAccount,
        "total_rotations", state.RotationCount)
    
    return nil
}
```

## Integration with caam
- Invoke `caam switch <provider> --next` to get next available account
- Parse caam JSON output for account details
- Handle case when no accounts are available

## Logging Requirements
- Log limit detection trigger with full event context
- Log current account before rotation
- Log new account after rotation
- Track total rotation count per pane
- Error logging with caam command details

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detect limit hits from LimitDetector events
- [ ] Invoke caam CLI for account switching
- [ ] Track rotation history per pane
- [ ] Handle caam errors gracefully
- [ ] Prevent rapid rotation loops (cooldown)
- [ ] Structured logging at each rotation step","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:01:42.400024504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T22:47:34.334499044Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T22:47:34.334476261Z","close_reason":"Implemented per-pane RotationState tracking, OnLimitHit handler with cooldown logic, and comprehensive tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-w254z","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-w254z","depends_on_id":"bd-3rngh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-whspn","title":"Task: Implement pre-compact context trigger","description":"## Overview
Implement the pre-compact trigger that detects when agents are approaching context limits and generates automatic handoffs BEFORE compaction occurs.

## Background & Reasoning
From Continuous-Claude-v3: The key insight is to capture context BEFORE compaction happens. Once compaction occurs, information is already lost. We need:
1. Context usage monitoring per agent
2. Threshold detection (70% = warn, 75%+ = auto-trigger)
3. Automatic handoff generation with agent context
4. Integration with dashboard for visibility

This is the core of "compound, don't compact" - we extract state and reload fresh instead of lossy compaction.

## Token Detection Mechanism

### Claude Code
- Read transcript size from `~/.claude/projects/{project}/session.jsonl`
- Estimate tokens: `file_size / 4` (rough approximation)
- Known limits: Claude = 200K tokens

### Codex CLI
- Parse context metrics from output (Codex reports usage)
- Monitor stderr for context warnings

### Gemini CLI
- Similar to Claude - monitor transcript/context file size

## Deliverables

### 1. Create internal/context/monitor.go
```go
package context

import (
    "log/slog"
    "os"
    "path/filepath"
    "sync"
    "time"
)

// Context limits by agent type (in tokens)
var DefaultLimits = map[string]int{
    "cc":  200000, // Claude 200K
    "cod": 128000, // Codex 128K
    "gmi": 1000000, // Gemini 1M
}

// Monitor tracks context window usage for agents
type Monitor struct {
    mu         sync.RWMutex
    agents     map[string]*AgentContext // key: paneID
    limits     map[string]int           // context limits by agent type
    logger     *slog.Logger
    projectDir string

    // Velocity tracking
    velocityWindow time.Duration
    history        map[string][]tokenSample
}

type tokenSample struct {
    tokens    int
    timestamp time.Time
}

type AgentContext struct {
    PaneID        string
    AgentType     string    // cc, cod, gmi
    SessionName   string
    CurrentTokens int
    MaxTokens     int
    LastUpdated   time.Time
    Velocity      float64   // tokens per minute (rolling average)
    TranscriptPath string   // path to agent's transcript file
}

// NewMonitor creates a context monitor with default limits
func NewMonitor(projectDir string) *Monitor {
    return &Monitor{
        agents:         make(map[string]*AgentContext),
        limits:         DefaultLimits,
        logger:         slog.Default().With("component", "context.monitor"),
        projectDir:     projectDir,
        velocityWindow: 5 * time.Minute,
        history:        make(map[string][]tokenSample),
    }
}

// RegisterAgent adds an agent to monitoring
func (m *Monitor) RegisterAgent(paneID, agentType, sessionName, transcriptPath string) {
    m.mu.Lock()
    defer m.mu.Unlock()

    maxTokens := m.limits[agentType]
    if maxTokens == 0 {
        maxTokens = 100000 // Default fallback
    }

    m.agents[paneID] = &AgentContext{
        PaneID:         paneID,
        AgentType:      agentType,
        SessionName:    sessionName,
        MaxTokens:      maxTokens,
        TranscriptPath: transcriptPath,
    }

    m.logger.Info("registered agent for context monitoring",
        "pane_id", paneID,
        "agent_type", agentType,
        "session", sessionName,
        "max_tokens", maxTokens,
    )
}

// Update records new context information for an agent
func (m *Monitor) Update(paneID string, tokens int) error {
    m.mu.Lock()
    defer m.mu.Unlock()

    agent, ok := m.agents[paneID]
    if !ok {
        return fmt.Errorf("agent not registered: %s", paneID)
    }

    oldTokens := agent.CurrentTokens
    agent.CurrentTokens = tokens
    agent.LastUpdated = time.Now()

    // Update velocity history
    m.history[paneID] = append(m.history[paneID], tokenSample{
        tokens:    tokens,
        timestamp: time.Now(),
    })
    // Trim old samples
    m.trimHistory(paneID)

    // Calculate velocity
    agent.Velocity = m.calculateVelocity(paneID)

    m.logger.Debug("updated context",
        "pane_id", paneID,
        "tokens", tokens,
        "delta", tokens-oldTokens,
        "velocity_tpm", agent.Velocity,
        "usage_pct", float64(tokens)/float64(agent.MaxTokens)*100,
    )

    return nil
}

// UpdateFromTranscript reads the transcript file and estimates tokens
func (m *Monitor) UpdateFromTranscript(paneID string) error {
    m.mu.RLock()
    agent, ok := m.agents[paneID]
    m.mu.RUnlock()

    if !ok {
        return fmt.Errorf("agent not registered: %s", paneID)
    }

    if agent.TranscriptPath == "" {
        return nil // No transcript to monitor
    }

    info, err := os.Stat(agent.TranscriptPath)
    if err != nil {
        if os.IsNotExist(err) {
            return nil // File doesn't exist yet
        }
        return err
    }

    // Estimate tokens from file size (rough: 4 bytes per token)
    estimatedTokens := int(info.Size() / 4)

    return m.Update(paneID, estimatedTokens)
}

// GetUsage returns context usage percentage for an agent
func (m *Monitor) GetUsage(paneID string) (float64, error) {
    m.mu.RLock()
    defer m.mu.RUnlock()

    agent, ok := m.agents[paneID]
    if !ok {
        return 0, fmt.Errorf("agent not registered: %s", paneID)
    }

    if agent.MaxTokens == 0 {
        return 0, nil
    }

    return float64(agent.CurrentTokens) / float64(agent.MaxTokens) * 100, nil
}

// GetAllUsage returns usage for all agents
func (m *Monitor) GetAllUsage() map[string]float64 {
    m.mu.RLock()
    defer m.mu.RUnlock()

    result := make(map[string]float64)
    for paneID, agent := range m.agents {
        if agent.MaxTokens > 0 {
            result[paneID] = float64(agent.CurrentTokens) / float64(agent.MaxTokens) * 100
        }
    }
    return result
}

// ShouldTriggerHandoff returns true if agent should create handoff
// Returns (should_trigger, reason)
func (m *Monitor) ShouldTriggerHandoff(paneID string) (bool, string) {
    m.mu.RLock()
    defer m.mu.RUnlock()

    agent, ok := m.agents[paneID]
    if !ok {
        return false, ""
    }

    usage := float64(agent.CurrentTokens) / float64(agent.MaxTokens) * 100

    // Hard threshold
    if usage >= 75 {
        return true, fmt.Sprintf("usage %.1f%% exceeds 75%% threshold", usage)
    }

    // Velocity-based prediction: if we'll hit 85% in 2 minutes
    if agent.Velocity > 0 {
        tokensIn2Min := agent.Velocity * 2
        predictedUsage := float64(agent.CurrentTokens+int(tokensIn2Min)) / float64(agent.MaxTokens) * 100
        if predictedUsage >= 85 {
            return true, fmt.Sprintf("velocity %.0f tpm predicts %.1f%% in 2 min", agent.Velocity, predictedUsage)
        }
    }

    return false, ""
}

// GetAgent returns agent context (for handoff metadata)
func (m *Monitor) GetAgent(paneID string) (*AgentContext, bool) {
    m.mu.RLock()
    defer m.mu.RUnlock()
    agent, ok := m.agents[paneID]
    if !ok {
        return nil, false
    }
    // Return copy to avoid race
    copy := *agent
    return &copy, true
}

func (m *Monitor) trimHistory(paneID string) {
    cutoff := time.Now().Add(-m.velocityWindow)
    history := m.history[paneID]
    var trimmed []tokenSample
    for _, s := range history {
        if s.timestamp.After(cutoff) {
            trimmed = append(trimmed, s)
        }
    }
    m.history[paneID] = trimmed
}

func (m *Monitor) calculateVelocity(paneID string) float64 {
    history := m.history[paneID]
    if len(history) < 2 {
        return 0
    }

    first := history[0]
    last := history[len(history)-1]
    duration := last.timestamp.Sub(first.timestamp).Minutes()
    if duration < 0.5 {
        return 0 // Not enough time span
    }

    tokenDelta := last.tokens - first.tokens
    if tokenDelta < 0 {
        return 0 // Tokens decreased (session reset?)
    }

    return float64(tokenDelta) / duration
}
```

### 2. Create internal/context/trigger.go
```go
package context

import (
    "log/slog"
    "sync"
    "time"

    "github.com/yourusername/ntm/internal/handoff"
)

// Trigger handles automatic handoff generation
type Trigger struct {
    monitor       *Monitor
    generator     *handoff.Generator
    writer        *handoff.Writer
    reader        *handoff.Reader
    logger        *slog.Logger

    mu            sync.Mutex
    lastTrigger   map[string]time.Time // paneID -> last trigger time
    cooldown      time.Duration        // minimum time between triggers per agent

    // Callbacks
    onTrigger     func(paneID, path string) // called when handoff generated
    onWarning     func(paneID string, usage float64) // called at warning threshold
}

// NewTrigger creates a pre-compact trigger
func NewTrigger(projectDir string, monitor *Monitor) *Trigger {
    return &Trigger{
        monitor:     monitor,
        generator:   handoff.NewGenerator(projectDir),
        writer:      handoff.NewWriter(projectDir),
        reader:      handoff.NewReader(projectDir),
        logger:      slog.Default().With("component", "context.trigger"),
        lastTrigger: make(map[string]time.Time),
        cooldown:    5 * time.Minute,
    }
}

// SetCallbacks registers callbacks for trigger events
func (t *Trigger) SetCallbacks(onTrigger func(string, string), onWarning func(string, float64)) {
    t.onTrigger = onTrigger
    t.onWarning = onWarning
}

// Check evaluates all agents and triggers handoffs as needed
// Returns list of generated handoff paths
func (t *Trigger) Check() ([]string, error) {
    t.logger.Debug("checking all agents for context triggers")

    var paths []string

    usage := t.monitor.GetAllUsage()
    for paneID, pct := range usage {
        // Warning callback at 70%
        if pct >= 70 && pct < 75 && t.onWarning != nil {
            t.onWarning(paneID, pct)
        }

        // Check if should trigger
        shouldTrigger, reason := t.monitor.ShouldTriggerHandoff(paneID)
        if !shouldTrigger {
            continue
        }

        // Check cooldown
        t.mu.Lock()
        lastTime := t.lastTrigger[paneID]
        if time.Since(lastTime) < t.cooldown {
            t.mu.Unlock()
            t.logger.Debug("skipping trigger due to cooldown",
                "pane_id", paneID,
                "last_trigger", lastTime,
                "cooldown", t.cooldown,
            )
            continue
        }
        t.lastTrigger[paneID] = time.Now()
        t.mu.Unlock()

        path, err := t.TriggerForAgent(paneID)
        if err != nil {
            t.logger.Error("failed to trigger handoff",
                "pane_id", paneID,
                "error", err,
            )
            continue
        }

        t.logger.Info("auto-handoff triggered",
            "pane_id", paneID,
            "reason", reason,
            "path", path,
        )

        paths = append(paths, path)

        // Callback
        if t.onTrigger != nil {
            t.onTrigger(paneID, path)
        }
    }

    return paths, nil
}

// TriggerForAgent forces handoff generation for specific agent
func (t *Trigger) TriggerForAgent(paneID string) (string, error) {
    agent, ok := t.monitor.GetAgent(paneID)
    if !ok {
        return "", fmt.Errorf("agent not found: %s", paneID)
    }

    t.logger.Debug("triggering handoff for agent",
        "pane_id", paneID,
        "session", agent.SessionName,
        "usage_pct", float64(agent.CurrentTokens)/float64(agent.MaxTokens)*100,
    )

    // Generate handoff from transcript if available
    var h *handoff.Handoff
    var err error

    if agent.TranscriptPath != "" {
        h, err = t.generator.GenerateFromTranscript(agent.SessionName, agent.TranscriptPath)
    } else {
        // Create minimal handoff with context info
        h = &handoff.Handoff{
            Session: agent.SessionName,
            Goal:    "Auto-handoff due to context limit",
            Now:     "Review and continue previous work",
            Status:  handoff.StatusPartial,
        }
    }

    if err != nil {
        t.logger.Warn("transcript generation failed, using minimal handoff",
            "pane_id", paneID,
            "error", err,
        )
        h = &handoff.Handoff{
            Session: agent.SessionName,
            Goal:    "Auto-handoff due to context limit",
            Now:     "Review and continue previous work",
            Status:  handoff.StatusPartial,
        }
    }

    // Add context metadata
    h.AgentID = paneID
    h.AgentType = agent.AgentType
    h.PaneID = paneID
    h.TokensUsed = agent.CurrentTokens
    h.TokensMax = agent.MaxTokens
    h.TokensPct = float64(agent.CurrentTokens) / float64(agent.MaxTokens) * 100

    // Enrich with git state
    t.generator.EnrichWithGitState(h)

    // Write auto-handoff
    path, err := t.writer.WriteAuto(h)
    if err != nil {
        return "", fmt.Errorf("write auto-handoff: %w", err)
    }

    // Invalidate reader cache
    t.reader.InvalidateCache()

    return path, nil
}

// StartPeriodicCheck starts a goroutine that checks at intervals
func (t *Trigger) StartPeriodicCheck(interval time.Duration, stop <-chan struct{}) {
    t.logger.Info("starting periodic context check",
        "interval", interval,
    )

    ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
    defer ticker.Stop()

    for {
        select {
        case <-ticker.C:
            if paths, err := t.Check(); err != nil {
                t.logger.Error("periodic check failed", "error", err)
            } else if len(paths) > 0 {
                t.logger.Info("periodic check generated handoffs",
                    "count", len(paths),
                )
            }
        case <-stop:
            t.logger.Info("stopping periodic context check")
            return
        }
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Monitor tracks token usage per agent
- [ ] Monitor registers agents with type, session, transcript path
- [ ] Update accepts token counts and updates velocity
- [ ] UpdateFromTranscript estimates tokens from file size
- [ ] Velocity calculated from rolling 5-minute window
- [ ] ShouldTriggerHandoff checks 75% threshold
- [ ] ShouldTriggerHandoff considers velocity prediction
- [ ] Trigger respects cooldown between triggers
- [ ] TriggerForAgent generates handoff with context metadata
- [ ] Check iterates all agents and returns generated paths
- [ ] Warning callback called at 70%
- [ ] Trigger callback called when handoff generated
- [ ] StartPeriodicCheck runs at configured interval
- [ ] All operations logged with structured slog
- [ ] Unit tests for monitor with various token scenarios
- [ ] Unit tests for velocity calculation
- [ ] Unit tests for trigger thresholds and cooldown
- [ ] Integration test with mock agents

- Callbacks allow dashboard integration without tight coupling
